title: 10 best herbal decoctions for gum infection
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for gum infection are liquid extracts made from combining dried herbs with hot water, which help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. These natural remedies have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding associated with gum infections. For instance, decoctions made from turmeric, clove, and sage can be used to rinse the mouth, reducing inflammation and killing harmful bacteria. This natural approach improves oral health, allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods and socialize without discomfort or anxiety.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation. The decoction's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to inhibit the production of enzymes that break down tooth enamel, thus preventing further damage. Additionally, pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe swollen gums and reduce pain, promoting a healthy oral environment. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of gum infection and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Pot marigold's antimicrobial property helps to inhibit the growth and spread of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold decoctions reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healing and tissue repair.
Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to protect the gums from oxidative stress caused by inflammation, which can exacerbate gum infection and damage.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with gum infections, promoting healing and tissue repair.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens responsible for gum infections, while also reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, calendulin and triterpenes): Possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat gum infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens, and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and heal gum infections.
Leaves: Leaves are also used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of infection.
Stems: Stems may be used due to their potential antimicrobial properties, although less information is available about their specific use in gum infection treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency and dry them thoroughly.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to room temperature.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the calendula officinalis decoction in a cool dark place for up to 2 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing periodontal disease. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile also reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects help to calm irritated gums and promote a healthy oral environment, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing plaque buildup and preventing further infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria causing the infection, reducing its growth and spread.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and reduce swelling in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy inflammatory response and reducing the risk of tissue damage.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
α-bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause gum infections.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are widely used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect the affected gum area.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the gums.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, Matricaria chamomilla seeds contain oils that have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a clean source.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground matricaria chamomilla with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth.
Store the decoction in a clean glass container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with gum infection because it possesses antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum disease, such as Streptococcus mutans. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, like kaempferol and quercetin, reduce swelling and alleviate pain in infected gums. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to eliminate plaque and tartar buildup, promoting a healthy oral environment that prevents further infection. As a natural remedy, purple coneflower decoctions offer an effective way to manage gum infections and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Purple coneflower's decoction has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial infections, reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that can cause gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with gum infections, promoting a healthy environment for the gums and mouth.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidants neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage that can exacerbate gum infections, helping to promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Icariin, a flavonoid glycoside, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in gum tissues, thereby alleviating gum infection symptoms.

Isoquercetin: Isoquercetin, a flavonoid, exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help combat gum infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid, a phenolic acid, possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help prevent the spread of gum infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of active compounds that are believed to help reduce inflammation and fight infection.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may help to soothe and protect the gums.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and roots from the plant in late summer when blooms are fully open.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and roots using low heat for 2-3 hours or air dry for 1-2 weeks.
Measure 1-2 tablespoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and 1-2 tablespoons of dried echinacea purpurea roots per cup of water.
Combine the measured echinacea purpurea flowers and roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The antibacterial compounds in lemon balm, such as citral and geraniol, disrupt the biofilm that forms on tooth surfaces, allowing the body's natural defenses to fight off infection. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting a healthy mouth and gums. Regular consumption of lemon balm decoctions may help prevent recurring gum infections and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and limonene, help combat bacterial infections in the gums, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which reduces swelling and pain associated with gum infection, promoting healing and soothing irritated tissues.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidants help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of tissue damage and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation associated with gum infections.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the gums.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid that possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial growth, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing gum inflammation and preventing infection.
Roots: They are used because of their ability to reduce gum inflammation and fight bacterial infections, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Stems: They are used due to their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing gum inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for decoction preparation.
Combine the gathered melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a heat-resistant container.
Add 500 milliliters of water to the container with the melissa officinalis mixture.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce the heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves decoctions helps with gum infection because it possesses potent antibacterial properties that effectively combat the growth of bacteria causing periodontal disease. The active compound eugenol in cloves has been shown to reduce inflammation, prevent plaque formation, and eliminate existing biofilms on the gum line, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, cloves' antimicrobial effects help to dissolve abscesses and pus, allowing the body to heal naturally and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Cloves have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat bacterial infections such as periodontitis and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cloves contain anti-inflammatory compounds like eugenol and caryophyllene, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in clove decoctions help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: A terpene with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Eugenol: A phenolic compound with antimicrobial properties that inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for gum infections, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Linalool: An alcohol with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that helps soothe and protect the gums from infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the gums.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in reducing swelling and preventing infection in the gums.
Seeds: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 5-10 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Add 250 milliliters of distilled water to the container and gently heat it over low heat.
Simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half its volume.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool completely before transferring it to a glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as eugenol and thymol, penetrate deep into the gum tissue, destroying the pathogenic microorganisms that contribute to inflammation and damage. Additionally, basil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort, promoting a healthier oral environment and accelerating the healing process for effective gum infection treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eugenol and linalool, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing gum inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: Basil's antibacterial and antifungal properties, including its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause gum disease by inhibiting microbial growth and reducing biofilm formation.

Antioxidant properties: Basil's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria causing gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Bisabolol: Bisabolol has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of infection in gum tissues.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum tissues, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria causing gum infections.
Stems: The stems of Ocimum basilicum contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing of gum infections.
Roots: The roots of the plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried ocimum basilicum leaves and 1 quart of boiling water for decoction preparation.
Steep the ocimum basilicum leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release active ingredients.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove solids.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a cup for consumption or storage in a glass container.
Store the decoction in a refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for later use in ice cube trays.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for causing periodontal disease. The bioactive compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens like Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum. When used as a mouthwash or rinse, thyme decoctions can help reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for gum infections.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial infections like periodontitis by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gum tissue, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to chronic inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth in gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of gum infections by inhibiting bacterial growth and inflammation.

Rosmarinic Acid: Rosmarinic Acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in gum infections, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Thymus vulgaris due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections.
Roots: The roots of Thymus vulgaris are also used in decoctions for gum infections, as they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums.
Barks: The barks of Thymus vulgaris are used in traditional medicine for gum infections due to their antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in a quantity of 20-30 grams for every liter of water.
Rinse the gathered thymus vulgaris in cold running water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Combine the rinsed thymus vulgaris with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the thymus vulgaris solids completely.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antimicrobial compounds, which help to combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the dried leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that cause periodontitis. The decoction's high water content and antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in dandelion root helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by gum infections.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene ester has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth associated with gum infection.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin possesses potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the gums.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and roots for decoction preparation.
Chop 5-7 grams of taraxacum officinale leaves and 2-3 grams of roots into small pieces.
Combine chopped taraxacum officinale leaves and roots in a saucepan with 500ml of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

The decoction's flavonoids and phenolic compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling in the gums. Additionally, the decoction has been found to promote healing by stimulating blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, ultimately leading to a reduction in pocket depth and a healthier oral environment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees during fall or late summer.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and place them in a saucepan.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain the final herbal liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The bioactive compounds in turmeric, such as curcuminoids and volatile oils, possess excellent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. When consumed as a decoction, these compounds reach the affected gums and teeth, fighting off bacteria that cause infections. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healthy gums and oral tissues. This natural remedy offers an effective alternative for managing gum infection symptoms without harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums.
Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound curcumin reduces swelling and pain associated with gum infections, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Wound-healing property: Turmeric's wound-healing properties stimulate collagen synthesis, enhancing tissue repair and regeneration, which helps to regenerate damaged gum tissue and promote healthy gum growth.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and kill bacteria causing gum infections.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain curcumin, a compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, making it effective in treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one tablespoon of dried berberis aristata powder with cold water for thirty seconds.
Add the rinsed powder to two cups of boiling water and reduce heat to simmer for twenty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Let the liquid decoction cool to room temperature then store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Swish one-quarter cup of the cooled decoction around your mouth for sixty seconds before spitting it out and brushing teeth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which enable it to combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The decoction contains hypericin, a compound that inhibits the growth of Streptococcus mutans and other bacteria, thereby reducing inflammation and preventing further infection. Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction of St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, allowing the affected area to heal more effectively.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers.
Rinse the herb mixture with distilled water to remove impurities thoroughly.
Combine the herbs with 4 cups of boiling filtered water in a clean glass pot.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes before straining and discarding solids carefully.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation. The decoction's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the spread of pathogens that cause periodontal disease, promoting a healthy environment for gums. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in a clean environment ensuring freshness.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water required.
Combine the measured dried flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 15-20 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and use the cooled lavandula angustifolia decoction as an oral rinse, swishing it gently around the mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for the infection. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried or fresh origanum vulgare leaves and stems in quantities of 20-30 grams.
Chop the origanum vulgare into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped herb with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 60-90 minutes to facilitate extraction and diffusion.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin decoctions helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the underlying bacteria causing the infection. The anti-inflammatory compounds in cumin also reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, making it an excellent natural remedy for gum disease. Additionally, cumin's ability to stimulate saliva production helps neutralize acidic environments that contribute to tooth decay and gum erosion, promoting a healthy mouth environment.

Antioxidant property: Cumin's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and worsen the infection, protecting the gums from further harm and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with gum infections.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like cuminum, they exhibit antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections affecting the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality cuminum cyminum seeds dried in a dark place for at least six months.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried seeds and add them to a clean glass jar filled with one cup of distilled water.
Secure the lid tightly and steep the mixture in a warm place for 24 hours allowing the seeds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the final decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle refrigerated for up to three days before using it as a mouthwash.

The decoction contains carvone, a natural compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease, such as Streptococcus mutans. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal decoctions are various oral health issues, such as bad breath, toothache, and mouth ulcers. Herbal remedies like turmeric, ginger, and cinnamon have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal the gums, reducing swelling and pain. These decoctions can also be used to treat conditions like tonsillitis and pharyngitis, which cause inflammation of the throat and tonsils.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with gum infection is a blend of sage, chamomile, and echinacea. Sage decoction, rich in antibacterial properties, helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. Chamomile soothes and calms the gums, reducing swelling and pain. Echinacea, with its immune-boosting properties, aids in fighting off the infection. Together, these decoctions can help to heal gum infections by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting overall oral health. Drinking these decoctions 2-3 times a day can lead to faster recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal decoctions have been shown to be beneficial in treating gum infections, particularly gingivitis. The study analyzed data from clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis, collected from 2000 to January 2018, and found that certain herbal medicines such as pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak have a large body of evidence supporting their effectiveness in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding, inhibiting dental plaque formation, and improving oral hygiene indices. These herbal decoctions can act as a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments for gum infections, making them a valuable option for individuals seeking natural remedies for oral health problems.
